user_story: As a user, I want to create a new project and select the desired Project Mode, so that my project is initialized according to my workflow requirements.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the low level document path when starting a new project, so that the system can correctly associate the necessary documentation with the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the low level document path when starting a new project, so that the system can correctly associate the required documentation with the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the paths for the low level document, high level document, and RTM file when starting a new project, so that all necessary files are correctly linked to the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user starting a new project, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method from Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the most suitable retrieval method for my project needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either 'Both Documents' or 'Low Level Document Only' (defaulting to 'Both Documents') when starting a new project, so that I can control the scope of keyword extraction.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user starting a new project, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Option (TF-IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when using Keyword Extraction methods, so that I can customize how keywords are weighted for my analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user starting a new project, I want to be required to select a Weight Threshold when I choose the IR Method 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can properly configure the information retrieval process.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user starting a new project, I want to optionally provide a Thesaurus and Stop Word filename, so that I can customize text processing according to my project's needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Thesaurus filename and a Stop Word file when starting a new project, so that I can customize text processing according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Feedback Method when starting a new project, so that I can customize my project's feedback approach.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel starting a new project and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created, so that I can avoid committing to unwanted changes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a new project and select its initial mode (Trace, Project, or Assess), so that the project is set up according to my workflow requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project with all its previous settings and modes, so that I can seamlessly continue my work from where I left off.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects or enter a new project name, so that I can easily load or create a project in the system.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a feedback method (currently only 'Standard Rochio'), so that I can customize how feedback is handled when loading a project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the load project process at any time and return to the previous activity, so that I am not forced to complete the action if I change my mind or made a mistake.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project by selecting its name and feedback method, so that I can continue working on my project from where I left off.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an RTM project by providing required parameters, so that I can initialize and assess requirements traceability efficiently.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to enter the new project name when using the LOAD RTM function, so that I can initialize a project with the correct identification.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path when loading an RTM, so that I can correctly set up a new project with all required documents.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the high level, low level, and RTM file paths when loading an RTM, so that the system can initialize the project with the correct documents for traceability analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the file path for the high-level requirements document and the RTM file, so that I can load the necessary files into RETRO for project setup.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally select an IR Method from Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can customize how RETRO processes the RTM according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either 'Both Documents' or 'Low Level Document Only' when loading an RTM, so that I can control the scope of keyword extraction according to my project needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Option (TF-IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when using Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can customize the weighting method for my information retrieval process.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user loading the RTM, I want to be required to select a Weight Threshold when I choose the IR Method 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can properly configure the retrieval process based on my input.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus and Stop Word filename when loading an RTM, so that I can customize the vocabulary processing for my project.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally provide a Thesaurus and Stop Word filename when loading the RTM, so that I can customize the vocabulary and filtering for my project.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user loading an RTM, I want to optionally be prompted to select a Feedback Method (currently only 'Standard Rochio'), so that I can customize the feedback process according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select the Project Mode as either 'Trace' or 'Assess' when loading an RTM, so that I can choose the appropriate mode for my project needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the LOAD RTM process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created, so that I can easily correct mistakes or change my mind without committing to a new project.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a new project with my chosen input fields and select either 'Trace' or 'Assess' mode, so that the project is initialized in the correct mode for my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to save the current data and status of my project, so that I can preserve my work and continue later without losing progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to prompt me to save my project when I close it, so that I don't lose any unsaved changes.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a user, I want to close the current project from the File menu without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can switch projects or continue working without restarting the application.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a user, I want to close the current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can switch between projects or stop working on one without leaving the app.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a user, I want to close the current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can continue working on other projects or tasks within the app.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to exit the RETRO application easily, so that I can finish my session and close the program when I am done working.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to select 'Show XML' from the Action menu, so that I can view the current project's high and low connections in XML format on a new screen.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a user, I want to view the XML version of the RTM by selecting the SHOW XML function, so that I can review the RTM data in its XML file format for verification or export purposes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a user, I want to modify the design of a generated report, so that I can customize the report layout and content to fit my specific needs.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the RTM report with the RTM With Text option from the Action menu, so that I can review detailed traceability information in a readable format.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the "Children with no parents" report from the Action menu, so that I can easily identify and document all children requirements that are not linked to any parent requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report” from the Action menu, so that I can easily review and document parent items without associated children.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Children with no parents report,” so that I can easily identify all child items that are not linked to any parent and ensure traceability in my project documentation process.  This helps maintain data integrity and supports compliance requirements during project reviews and audits.  I should be able to access this report from the GENERATE REPORT function and save it for future reference or analysis, in a format that is easy to read and share, so that I
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report” from the Action menu, so that I can easily review and document requirements that lack parent associations in a readable format.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save a "Not satisfied requirements with text report" so that I can easily review and document requirements that have not been fulfilled in a readable format for further analysis or action steps during the project lifecycle.  This helps ensure all unmet requirements are tracked and addressed efficiently.  [3.8.2.7]  [GENERATE REPORT]  [Related: RTM report, Parent with no children report]  [Checkbox for Not Satisfied Requirement]  [New screen for Not S., so that I
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report” so that I can easily review unmet requirements with detailed information for further analysis and action steps after a project assessment or trace process is completed. [3.8.2.7] [GENERATE REPORT] [Related US] [3.8.2.6] [Action menu] [Checkbox option] [Text report] [Save] [Review] [Analysis] [Process results] [User] [Project] [Requirements] [Assessment] [Trace] [Report] [XML alternative] [Easy to , so that [
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on the most relevant information and avoid missing any important links.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to choose whether the filter applies to a specific high-level document element (LOCAL) or all high-level elements (GLOBAL), so that I can control the scope of my requirement filtering.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a user, I want to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a weight cutoff between 0 and 1.0, so that I can focus on the most relevant requirements for my analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a user, I want to specify the number of top elements to display using a 'Show Top' option and input box, so that I can focus on the most relevant low-level requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to activate a Kill Hidden Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed, so that my workspace only shows relevant, visible requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to view the text of low-level requirements, so that I can understand and analyze each requirement in detail.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low-level documents by Requirement ID, so that I can easily locate and organize requirements for review and analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low-level documents by their weights, so that I can easily prioritize and analyze requirements based on their importance.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low level documents by their original document order, so that I can review them in the sequence they were initially created or provided.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user, I want to provide feedback on the tracing and assessing results, so that the system can improve its algorithms and offer more accurate process improvements.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a user, I want to quickly identify and remove low-level requirements with a status of 'No Link', so that I can efficiently manage and clean up unlinked requirements in my projects.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a user, I want to load and work on multiple projects simultaneously, so that I can efficiently switch between ongoing tasks without closing my current work.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a user, I want to close a specific project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can manage multiple open projects efficiently and keep working on others.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a user, I want to import projects into the RETRO System, so that I can quickly start working with existing data in Trace mode.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide file paths for high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can import the necessary files into the RETRO system efficiently.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want the RETRO System's GUI to display all labels and documents with correct spelling, so that I can clearly understand and trust the information presented.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a user, I want the RETRO System to provide a GUI mechanism for interaction, so that I can easily access and use its features without needing command-line knowledge.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirements using keyword search in browse mode, so that I can quickly locate and associate relevant requirements.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to find matching low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the specific requirement I need.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to find high-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the information I need for manual linking or review.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to manually delete recommended links marked as 'No Link', so that I can ensure only valid links remain in the system.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on the most relevant information during manual linking and tracing.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to automatically suggest candidate links between high and low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently trace and validate requirement relationships.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want RETRO to provide a mechanism to generate a trace on all requirements, so that I can efficiently review and evaluate the traceability across the entire project.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate a trace for specific requirements in RETRO, so that I can evaluate candidate links between selected high and low-level requirements efficiently.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a user, I want to mark the tracing process as completed, so that the project is finalized and no further changes can be made to the trace results.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a user, I want to manually link or unlink candidate requirements in the TRACING RESULTS, so that I can correct or refine the traceability when RETRO's suggestions do not match my expectations.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a user, I want to save the current status of my trace, so that I can preserve my progress and continue later if needed.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to allow me to save the current status of my trace, so that I can preserve my progress and continue my work later without losing any information.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a user, I want to finish the tracing process and have the project marked as completed, so that no further changes can be made and the trace results are finalized.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to automatically save all current data and the project when I close the current project, so that I do not lose any progress during the tracing process.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a user, I want to provide feedback on the tracing results after running an accuracy and completeness check, so that future tracing can be improved based on my input.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to run an accuracy and completeness check by comparing RETRO IR method results to the RTM, so that I can identify and improve any missing or incorrect requirement links.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to see a list of recommendations generated by RETRO's TRACING process, so that I can review and provide feedback on the suggested requirement links.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to display or hide the recommendations list in ASSESS mode, so that I can control when I view suggestions based on RETRO’s TRACING process.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to update the satisfaction status of high-level requirements as 'Satisfied', 'Not Satisfied', or 'Partially Satisfied', so that I can accurately reflect the current fulfillment level based on linked low-level requirements.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display the percentage level of satisfaction for each candidate link after running the Satisfaction Analysis, so that I can quickly assess how well each low-level requirement satisfies the high-level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to delete all low-level requirements that are not linked to any high-level requirement, so that I can efficiently clean up irrelevant or orphaned requirements from my project.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a user, I want to select and delete individual recommended links marked as 'No Link', so that I can efficiently manage and clean up unlinked low-level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to remove all low-level requirements that are not linked to any high-level requirement, so that I can efficiently clean up unlinked requirements and maintain traceability.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to select and delete any recommended link, including those marked as 'No Link', so that I can ensure only relevant and accurate links are maintained in the system.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want each candidate link to be color-coded based on its satisfaction status, so that I can quickly and easily identify the satisfaction level of each link at a glance.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want each candidate link to be color-coded based on its satisfaction status, so that I can quickly and easily identify the satisfaction level of each link at a glance.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
